titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Lefferts Family Papers
titlestmt.author: compiled by Janet Elaine Gertz

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid created in accordance with manuscripts and archives' processing manual.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday August 30, 2018 at 21:50

did.unittitle: Lefferts family papers
did.abstract: Financial papers, deeds, legal documents, bills and receipts and other papers relating to the Lefferts and related families of New York. The papers include a group of documents concerning Aaron Burr's efforts in 1833-1834 to obtain a government pension. The papers also contain newspaper clippings covering the period 1830-1936.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Lefferts Family Papers (MS 319). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Mrs. Mario Pagano and Mrs. John Peter Beck, 1955.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Financial and real estate papers, legal documents, correspondence, and other papers relating to the Lefferts family of New York City and vicinity, and dating from 1712 to 1850, make up this collection. Various descendants of Jacob Lefferts (1686-1768), fourth son of Leffert Pietersen van Haughwout, a Huguenot immigrant from North Holland to New York in 1660, are represented. For a chart outlining the genealogical relations of the Lefferts family, please consult theGenealogical Chart. Further details can be found in Genealogy of the Lefferts Family, 1650-1878, by Teunis G. Bergen, 1878.

Also included in the papers are a group of documents concerning Aaron Burr's efforts to obtain a government pension in 1833-1834 for his Revolutionary War services. While most of the documents are in the hand of his clerk or of people making depositions, a few are signed by Burr.
The contents of the collection have been arranged chronologically and a brief description of each item provided. Most of the papers are in very fragile condition and require careful handling.
The papers were the gift of Mrs. Mario Pagano and Mrs. John Peter Beck in 1955.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Various descendants of Jacob Lefferts (1686-1768), a fourth son of Leffert Pietersen van Haughwout, a Huguenot immigrant from North Holland to New York in 1660, are represented.
For a chart outlining the genealogical relations of the Lefferts family, please consult theGenealogical Chart.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Unpublished materials authored or otherwise produced by the creator(s) of this collection are in the public domain. There are no restrictions on use. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

geogname.part: United States -- History -- Colonial period, ca. 1600-1775

did.unittitle: Inventory
Lott, Abraham: transfer of land in King's County to Marten Adriaense (in Dutch)
Remsen, Rem: bond to Jeremiah Remsen
Van der Beek, Rem: bond with Christofell Van der Beek to Ida Van Wickelen
Rapelye, Jannetie (Lefferts): receipt to executors of her husband Jeronemus Rapely's estate
Raypelye, Jannetie (Lefferts): receipt to executors of her husband Jeronemus Rapely's estate for money to maintain her son Jeronemus Rapelye
Rapelye, Jeronemus estate: receipts from various persons to Rem Remsen and Nicholaes Veghate, executors
Lefferts, Barent: pocket account book, mostly bland
Berdeau, Peter: bill of sale for a slave to Barent Lefferts
Andriese, Andries: bond to Gerret Middach
Lefferts, Barent: bond to Gerrit Noorstrant
Wyckoff, Femmetie (Mrs. Cornelius): receipt to Hendrick Eldert, Nicholas Wyckoff and Rem Remsen, executors, for a legacy under the will of Hendrick Wyckoff
Conditions of sale of a plantation, Bedford, King's County
Bloom, Jacob: order on Barent Lefferts in favor of Barent Andriese
Elder, J.: ALS to Mr. Lefferts, Charlestown, [Mass.]
Gerritsen, Samuel W.: Als to Barent Lefferts
Miller, Josiah: receipt to Barent Lefferts
Remsen, Captain: order to freeholders of Brooklyn to supply their quota of wood
Lefferts, Barent: Promissory note to Jacob van der Bilt
Simons, Andrew: quit claim to Gerrit Wyckoff, Jacob Bloom, and Barent Lefferts, executors of the estate of Andress Andressen
New York (State), tax collector: receipt for carriage tax to Barent Lefferts
Lefferts, Rem: sundry farm and stock-raising accounts
Washington Insurance Co., New York: insurance policy to Abigail Fowler
Spader, William: bond to executors of the estate of Michal Vandervoort
Ryerson, Jacob, Jr.: agreement with Rem and Maria Lefferts for maintenance of a fence
Remsen, Jacob: two bonds to Johannes Remsen
Lefferts, Rem: "An account of the blacks work and monies" received from R. Lefferts
Washington Insurance Co., New York: insurance policy to George L. Cornell
Lefferts, Rem: lease agreement with Abraham Brown for land between Bedford and Flatubush
Lefferts, Rem: lease agreement with Peter C. Bell for house and land in Brooklyn
Edsall, Daniel: agreement as apprentice to John Degraw to learn house carpentry
Burr, Aaron: papers dealing with Burr's efforts to secure a pension
Cuppin, S.: ALS to Nelson Chase, Worcester
Lefferts, Rem: lease agreement with Charles Basham for house and land in Bedford
Warmington, William: certificate with Louis Dufland to the condition of a mare sold to Mr. Lefferts
Halsey, Frederick: receipt to Rem Lefferts
Incomplete deed for land in Brooklyn to Rem and Maria Lefferts
Lefferts, Rem deed to Cornelius W. Van Brunt for land in Flatbush, King's County
Burtis, Stilwell Co.: bill to Rem Lefferts
Lefferts, Rem: lease agreement with William L. Haskins for house and land in Brooklyn
Whitson, Silas: receipt to Rem Lefferts in a horse trade
Contribution Fire Insurance Co.: certificate of policy to Abraham and Henry Brown
Lefferts Rem: bond to David Carll
Baisley, John: receipt to Mr. Sexton
Brooklyn Horse Guards: printed invitation to annual ball
Murphy, John G.: deed to Rem Lefferts for land in Brooklyn
Lefferts, Rem: bond to Rufus R. Skeel
Lefferts, Rem: lease agreement with James Powell
Skeel, Rufus R.: assignment to Philip Dater, guardian of the infant children of Theron and Eliza Skeel, of a mortgage against Rem and Maria Lefferts
Disbrow, S.M.: ALS to his uncle Rem Lefferts, Sr.
Rapelye, George: declaration with John Purdy of errors in description of property to be released to Rem Lefferts
Olcott, McKesson Co.: bill to Rem Lefferts
Sloane, William: bill for a rug to R. Lefferts
Troy House: bill to Mr. Lefferts
Long Island Bank: notice to Rem Lefferts of note due
Lefferts, Rem: itinerary New York to Albany with L. Lefferts
Newspaper clippings
